message:
Redesign tweet wall with glassmorphic Frankenstein theme and react-tweet

Full rewrite of tweet-wall.tsx with three-layer architecture:
  - FallbackContent: bare text/stats for react-tweet's fallback prop,
    rendered *without* outer chrome to prevent double-nesting when
    react-tweet wraps it in its own container
  - GlassFrankenCard: shared glassmorphic shell with FrankenBolts at
    corners, FrankenStitch top/bottom seams, BorderBeam on hover,
    green glow border, and backdrop-blur-md glass effect
  - EmbeddedTweetCard / FallbackCard: motion.div wrappers that
    provide staggered whileInView entrance animations

  react-tweet integration: When a tweet has a tweetId, the card
  renders a real Twitter embed via <ReactTweet> with full media
  support (images, video, quoted tweets). Falls back gracefully to
  FallbackContent for tweets without IDs.

  Visual details: floating alien artifact Image decoration on XL
  screens, engagement stats row (replies, reposts, likes, views,
  bookmarks) with proper icons, "View Source" link to original post.

CSS overrides for react-tweet (app/globals.css):
  90 lines of CSS custom property overrides that retheme the
  react-tweet component to match the Frankenstein aesthetic:
  - Transparent background (glass effect shows through)
  - All blue accents replaced with green-500/green-400
  - Verified badge color: green instead of Twitter blue
  - Media images get rounded corners + green glow on hover, but
    profile avatars are excluded via :not([class*="avatar"])
  - Links forced green, timestamps set to monospace
  - Action bar (like/reply/retweet) subtle at 70% opacity, full
    on card hover

Alien artifact asset (public/images/frankentui-alien-artifact.webp):
  Converted from frankentui_alien_artifact.png via cwebp at quality
  85. Shows as a thumbnail in the tweet section header alongside
  the "Hey, it fell off the back of a Spaceship" quote.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
